findings.requirement: US-AC5 / edge case: rate=0 (unlimited) must not pathologically spin a busy-loop; reuse the same loop idiom as rollbacks/tempfiles
findings.detail: User-spec edge case (line 145-146) requires that rate=0 NOT degenerate into a pathological busy-loop and reuse the rollbacks/tempfiles loop idiom. Tech-spec Decision 2 implements unlimited via rate.NewLimiter(rate.Inf,1) so Allow() is always true, and the Risks table explicitly acknowledges this 'spins a tight loop (pegs a CPU core)' and labels it 'intended'. This is a conscious divergence from the user-spec's wording: the spec asked to avoid pathological busy-looping, the tech-spec accepts a CPU-pegging loop. Reasonable for a 'killer' workload, but the resolution should be made explicit (a Decision noting the spec's busy-loop concern is intentionally accepted) rather than living only in the Risks table.

findings.requirement: Edge case: very large plan-size makes a single PREPARE heavy/slow — behavior to be documented
findings.detail: User-spec edge case (line 135-136) asks to document that a very large plan-size can make an individual PREPARE heavy/slow. Tech-spec Decision 4 and the Risks table cover plan-size tuning and document the default as empirical, but neither the README task (Task 3) nor a Decision explicitly carries the instruction to document the 'very large plan-size -> heavy/slow single PREPARE' caveat for end users. Minor documentation-coverage gap.

summary: 12/14 requirements fully covered, 2 partial, 0 missing. All 14 user-spec acceptance criteria map to tech-spec coverage (flags+envars/defaults, single dedicated db.Connect, unique PREPARE + monotonic counter, plan-size target-list heaviness + default 1000, rate throttle with 0=unlimited, escalation panel via Infof with optional show-memory PG14+ and graceful PG<14 degradation, connect-failure-as-init-error vs mid-run climax, PREPARE-error Warnf+continue, connection-loss climax returning nil, clean ctx stop, Conninfo never logged, integration mechanics test without real OOM, validate() table test, README + impact=Yes). Reverse traceability: no unauthorized scope additions — every tech-spec element traces to a requirement; tests/README/CLI wiring are required infra. Right-sizing: NOT overengineered — Decisions 2,3,5,6 each reuse a single loop/code path and reject the more complex alternatives (no pgx error typing, no external monitoring polling, no separate unlimited branch); auto plan-size was deliberately removed (user-spec Decision) and correctly absent from the tech-spec. NOT underengineered — connect failure, mid-run PREPARE error, connection-loss climax, ctx cancel, show-memory read failure, and PG<14 are all handled. Solution and Architecture have real technical substance (concrete PREPARE builder, rate.Inf mechanism, single-connection rationale). Tasks (4, embedded in tech-spec) are brief scope descriptions with files/verify, no pseudocode — cover package, CLI wiring, README, and QA. Two minor partials: rate=0 busy-loop acceptance and the very-large-plan-size doc caveat. No critical findings -> pass.
